usage: editResize [-help] [-keep_center_line {0 1 auto}] [-no_conn] [-polygon_edge_start {x y} | -to <float>] {[[[-direction {x y diag45 diag135} -side {high low}] | [-polygon_edge_start {x y} -polygon_edge_end {x y}]] [-offset <float> | -to <float>]] | [-corner {low_left low_right up_left up_right} -dx <float> -dy <float>]}

options.name: -corner
options.description: {low_left low_right up_left up_right} Specifies the start corner to stretch the wire

options.name: -dx
options.description: Specifies the distance to stretch the wire in x direction.

options.name: -dy
options.description: Specifies the distance to stretch the wire in y direction.

options.name: -keep_center_line
options.description: Specifies whether resize the pin/wire with center line unchanged.

options.name: -no_conn
options.description: Specifies whether resize the wire without auto via/wire creation and DRC.

options.name: -offset
options.description: Specifies the distance to change the wire size, in microns.

options.name: -polygon_edge_end
options.description: End point for the polyon edge {x y}. This is only used to resize a polygon.

options.name: -polygon_edge_start
options.description: Start point for the polyon edge {x y}. This is only used to resize a polygon.

options.name: -side
options.description: Specifies the enlarge/shrink side. High means enlarge/shrinks the wire from top or right. Low means enlarge /shrinks the wire from bottom or left.
